---
title: "Automating iLand on HPC"
subtitle: "From hard-coded scripts to parallel, replicated scenario runs"
from: markdown+emoji
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [dark, custom.scss]
    highlight-style: monokai
    slide-number: c/t
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    code-block-height: 520px
    smaller: true
---

## Parallel iLand runs on HPC

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

**When this is necessary:**

- running replicates in parallel with a JavaScript dependency (e.g., saving fire outputs).
- running on an HPC system where iLand's internal output structure needs redirecting.

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

**Why this is necessary:**

- efficient use of HPC resources.
- With our 60 kha landscape, individual runs take 4–8 hours at fine sapling resolution, but only use around a third of a node.
- A single job on Derecho books the whole node's resources.

:::

:::

The original scripts were legacy scripts with hard-coded pathways not intended for HPC use in parallel.

. . .

::: {.callout-note}
Not necessary if you do not have a JavaScript dependency or if you can run replicates sequentially.
:::

---

## This hurt...

![](/images/github.png){fig-align="center"}

---

## Original JavaScript

_The origin of all evil lies here._

**JavaScript file**

```javascript
function afterFireProcessing() {
   var praefix = Globals.year;
   var pid = Fire.id;
   var nFire = Fire.grid('nFire');
   // save the form of the fire in ESRI raster format
   // save a file for each fire
   Fire.gridToFile(
     'crownkill',
     '/glade/derecho/scratch/user/output/CPCRW_nofecund/crownkill/crownkill_'
     + pid + '_' + praefix + '.txt'
   );
}
```

**Project XML tag**:

```xml
<management>
  <enabled>true</enabled>
  <file>saveWorkflow.js</file>
</management>
```

---

## Original bash script

**Bash script** to run project file:

```bash
reps=1
years=100

for xml in $xml_list
do
    # extract filename, no extension
    dirname=$(basename $xml | cut -d"." -f 1)
    for rep in $(seq 1 $reps)
    do
        /glade/work/user/iLand2.0/iland-model/build/ilandc/ilandc \
          /glade/work/user/CPCRW_fecund/$dirname.xml $years \
          system.database.out=${dirname}_$rep.sqlite
        cp -r /glade/derecho/scratch/user/output/${dirname}/crownkill/* \
          /glade/derecho/scratch/user/output/${dirname}/crownkill_$dirname.$rep/
        rm /glade/derecho/scratch/user/output/${dirname}/crownkill/*
    done
done
```

. . .

::: {.callout-warning}
to run different scenarios required duplicating the project file, the .js file, correcting the pathway references, and manually creating an output directory sctructure.
:::


---

## Remove hard-coding from JavaScript

**Before**

```javascript
function afterFireProcessing() {
   var praefix = Globals.year;
   var pid = Fire.id;
   var nFire = Fire.grid('nFire');
   Fire.gridToFile(
     'crownkill',
     '/glade/derecho/scratch/user/output/CPCRW_nofecund/crownkill/crownkill_'
     + pid + '_' + praefix + '.txt'
   );
}
```

**After**

```javascript
function afterFireProcessing() {
   var outputPath = Globals.path(
     Globals.setting('system.path.output')
   );
   var praefix = Globals.year;
   var pid = Fire.id;
   var nFire = Fire.grid('nFire');

   Fire.gridToFile(
     'crownkill',
     outputPath + '/crownkill/crownkill_' + pid + '_' + praefix + '.txt'
   );
   nFire.save(outputPath + '/nFire/nFire_' + pid + '.asc');
}
```

---

`Globals.setting('system.path.output')` reflects tags in the project file.

JavaScript:

```javascript
function afterFireProcessing() {
   var outputPath = Globals.path(
     Globals.setting('system.path.output')
   );
   ...
}
```

Project xml:

```xml
<project>
  <system>
    <path>
     ...
	  <output>/glade/derecho/scratch/qasena/output_auto/CPCRW_hist</output>
    </path>
     ...

```
---

## CSV-based replicates & scenarios

The CSV looks like this — one row per scenario:

<br>

| fecund | gcm | fri | epsilon | dbh | id |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| sp_param | NorEsm-MMssp126 | 120 | 2.7 | 2.5 | filt_1980 |
| sp_param | TaiEsm-MMssp126 | 120 | 2.7 | 2.5 | filt_1980 |

<br>

::: {.callout-note}
# CSV scenario management documented in iLand book
Approach adapted from the [iLand book](https://iland-model.org/iland-book/Managing_simulation_experiments.html) — note there are a few bugs in the examples there.
:::

---

## Defensive XML defaults

I set placeholder values in the project file tags so iLand errors loudly rather than silently falling back to leftover inputs:

```xml
<database>
  <in>species_params_overwritten_by_csv</in>
  <out>out_overwritten_by_csv</out>
  <climate>gcm_overwritten_by_csv</climate>
</database>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
If a CSV value is missing or mis-spelled, iLand will fail to find the file and exit — rather than quietly reusing a stale database from a previous run.
:::

---

## New bash script

The script reads the CSV line by line and creates subdirectories like `crownkill/` to save fire outputs, and cleans up after itself:

```bash
xml=$1  start_rep=$2  end_rep=$3

path="/glade/work/qasena/iLand2.0/iland-model/build/ilandc/ilandc"
output_path="/glade/derecho/scratch/qasena/output_auto/CPCRW_play"
simulation_years="5"
csv_name="/glade/work/qasena/iLand_automated/iland_reps.csv"

mkdir -p "${output_path}"

sed 1d $csv_name | while IFS=, read -r fecund gcm fri
do
    scenario_dir="${output_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}"
    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir/log"

    for rep in $(seq $start_rep $end_rep)
    do
        mkdir -p ${scenario_dir}/crownkill_${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}
        "${path}" ${xml} $simulation_years \
            system.database.out=${gcm}_${fecund}/output_${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}.sqlite \
            system.database.climate=${gcm}.sqlite \
            system.database.in=${fecund}.sqlite \
            system.logging.logFile=${scenario_dir}/log/log_${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}.txt \
            modules.fire.fireReturnInterval=${fri}

        if compgen -G "${output_path}/crownkill/*" > /dev/null; then
            cp -r ${output_path}/crownkill/* \
                  ${scenario_dir}/crownkill_${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}
            rm ${output_path}/crownkill/*
        fi
    done
done
```

---

## {.big-text}

[**Wonderful!**]{style="font-size:2em;"}

---

## {.big-text}

[**Beautiful!**]{style="font-size:4em;"}

. . .

![](/images/github_working.png)

. . .

[**Amazing!**]{style="font-size:6em;"}


---

## {.big-text}

[**FAILURE!!!**]{style="font-size:8em;"}

---

## The race condition problem

This approach worked in serial during local testing. But running replicates in parallel means multiple iLand instances share the same `crownkill/` folder simultaneously.

```bash
# ❌ v1 approach — breaks with parallel instances
"${path}" ${xml} $years \
    system.database.out=output_${rep}.sqlite …
cp -r ${output_path}/crownkill/* \
      ${scenario_dir}/crownkill_${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}/
rm    ${output_path}/crownkill/*
```

. . .

::: {.callout-warning}
# Race conditions
When multiple iLand instances run simultaneously they all write fire grids to
the **same** `crownkill/` directory. The `cp` of rep 1 can pick up files from
rep 2, and the `rm` can delete files that rep 3 hasn't copied yet.
:::

---

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

[:sob:]{style="font-size:4em;"}

[:sob:]{.fragment style="font-size:8em;"}

[:sob:]{.fragment style="font-size:10em;"}

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {style="text-align: right;"}

[:sob:]{.fragment style="font-size:7em;"}

[:sob:]{.fragment style="font-size:9em;"}

[:sob:]{.fragment style="font-size:11em;"}

:::

:::

:::

---

I tried to overwrite the `<output>` tag with variations on: `system.path.output`. To no avail, iLand's internal path resolution doesn't seem to allow this tag to be overridden at runtime? **Kristin!! Help!!**

```xml
<project>
  <system>
    <path>
      <home></home>
      <database>databases</database>
	    <temp>temp</temp>
      <lip>lip</lip>
      <script>scripts</script>
	    <init>init</init>
>>>>>><output>/glade/derecho/scratch/qasena/output_auto/CPCRW_hist</output>
    </path>
      ...
```

. . .

I've gone and made the JavaScript file to resolve to `<output>` so that I don't need to duplicate the file for each scenario!

---


## Final solution: per-replicate XML

The fix: give each replicate its *own temporary XML* with its output path baked in.

::: {.callout-note}
# This was written by AI, I think it's correct:
iLand resolves all output paths relative to the `<output>` tag in the project XML. By overriding it per-replicate, the `.js` file's `Globals.setting('system.path.output')` resolves to the correct scratch directory — with no shared state between instances.
:::

```bash
scenario_dir="${output_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}/rep_${rep}"
tmp_xml="${xml_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}_${rep}.xml"
# 1. copy the base project XML
cp "$xml" "$tmp_xml"
# 2. rewrite the <output> tag to an absolute, replicate-specific scratch path
sed -i "s|<output>.*</output>|<output>${scenario_dir}</output>|" "$tmp_xml"
# 3. run iLand with the patched XML
"${path}" "$tmp_xml" "$simulation_years" …
# 4. clean up the temporary XML
rm "$tmp_xml"
```

. . .

::: {.callout-tip}
# Now
Each iLand instance writes fire grids directly to its own `scenario_dir/crownkill/` — no shared directory, no race conditions.
:::

---

## Complete script: setup

```bash
set -e           # terminate if non-zero exit
set -u           # terminate if variable unset
set -o pipefail  # terminate if any command in pipeline fails

# Arguments
xml=$1
start_rep=$2
end_rep=$3

# Site-specific variables — edit these for your environment
path="/glade/work/qasena/iLand2.0/iland-model/build/ilandc/ilandc"
output_path="/glade/derecho/scratch/qasena/output_auto/CPCRW_hist_spinup"
xml_path="/glade/work/qasena/iLand_automated"
simulation_years="300"
csv_name="/glade/work/qasena/iLand_automated/iland_reps.csv"

mkdir -p "${output_path}"
```

---

## Complete script: loop & directories

```bash
# Read CSV (skip header) and loop through scenarios × replicates
sed 1d "$csv_name" | while IFS=, read -r fecund gcm fri epsilon dbh id
do
    for rep in $(seq "$start_rep" "$end_rep")
    do
        echo "running gcm $gcm with fecund $fecund rep $rep"

        scenario_dir="${output_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}/rep_${rep}"
>>>>>>  tmp_xml="${xml_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}_${rep}.xml"

        mkdir -p "${scenario_dir}/crownkill"
        mkdir -p "${scenario_dir}/nFire"
        mkdir -p "${scenario_dir}/log"

        # Patch a per-replicate XML with a unique output path
>>>>>>  cp "$xml" "$tmp_xml"
>>>>>>  sed -i "s|<output>.*</output>|<output>${scenario_dir}</output>|" "$tmp_xml"
```

---

## Complete script: run & cleanup

```bash
        # Run iLand model
        "${path}" "$tmp_xml" "$simulation_years" \
            system.database.out=${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}_${rep}.sqlite \
            system.database.climate=${gcm}.sqlite \
            system.database.in=${fecund}.sqlite \
            system.logging.logFile=${scenario_dir}/log/log_${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}_${rep}.txt \
            modules.fire.fireReturnInterval=${fri} \
            model.settings.epsilon=${epsilon} \
            output.saplingdetail.minDbh=${dbh}

>>>>>>  rm "$tmp_xml"

    done
done
```

---

## Summary

| Problem | Solution |
|---|---|
| Hard-coded paths in `.js` file | Read output path from `Globals.setting('system.path.output')` |
| Hard-coded  output paths in bash script | Relative to project root |
| One xml and JavaScript per scenario | CSV-based scenarios |
| Directory creation was manual | `mkdir -p` inside the replicate loop |
| Race conditions (shared crownkill dir) | Per-replicate temporary XML with unique `<output>` path |

. . .

::: {.callout-warning}
# Did I spend a lot of time being stupid?
Was there a better way to do all this to begin with?
:::

## Communing with Derecho

- Git version control for all scripts, CSV, and project files
- Include a model tracking csv:

<br>

::: {style="font-size:0.55em;"}

| date_run_ymd | job_id | success | time_taken_h:m | walltime_reqested_h | param_notes | git_head |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 20260224 | 2532399 | TRUE | 4.6 | 24 | Saving yr > 260 | 1e3d9e8 |
| 20260224 | 2532378 | TRUE | 4.57 | 24 | Saving yr > 260 | 1e4e7d9 |

:::

<br>

- I edit the scenario csv or project file -> `git push` to cloud -> `git pull` to Derecho -> record the git head (`git show HEAD`) -> submit the job, and record the jobid for every submission in my model tracking csv.

- This way I can easily trace back from an output to the exact code and parameters that generated it.

## Next?

- Questions and comments on the approach?
- Containerization?
- Workflow for initialising multiple landscapes in Alaska?

::: {.callout-note}
# Scripts in GitHub repo!
I've included the scenario csv, bash script, JavaScript file, and XML in [iLand-NA-parallel repo](https://github.com/QuinnAsena/iLand-NA-parallel).

- If you want to use them or suggest improvements, please open a PR!

- I'm transferring the inherited xml file to the maximilist project file.

:::
